claims.quote: The Academic Visitor Program coordinates observerships at Hospital for Special Surgery for physicians, students, and allied health care professionals.
claims.claimText: HSS Academic Visitor Program coordinates observerships for physicians, students, and allied health care professionals.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Page describes observerships open to physicians and students — pre-residency USCE access.

claims.quote: Observerships are informal, non-accredited visits, designed for individuals wishing to observe surgical and clinical care at HSS.
claims.claimText: Observerships are informal, non-accredited visits to observe surgical and clinical care at HSS.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Defines the observership as non-accredited clinical/surgical observation — Tier 1 USCE.

claims.quote: Observerships range from one day to two months and are largely structured according to the desires of the visitor and hosting physician.
claims.claimText: Observerships range from one day to two months, structured by visitor and host.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit duration range for the observership program.

claims.quote: The Academic Visitor Program coordinates observerships at HSS for clinicians, students and allied healthcare professionals. It is open to all persons inside or outside of the United States who:
claims.claimText: Open to all persons inside or outside of the United States — international eligibility.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility explicitly extends to persons outside the United States — IMG-relevant Tier 1 signal.

claims.quote: Are 18 years of age or older.
claims.claimText: Applicants must be 18 years of age or older.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Age eligibility criterion for the observership.

claims.quote: Can provide proof of current COVID-19 and influenza vaccination, including medical documents outlined in the health guidelines.
claims.claimText: Must provide proof of current COVID-19 and influenza vaccination.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Immunization requirement is a Tier 1 USCE eligibility detail.

claims.quote: Who have found a confirmed HSS host and have a serious interest in musculoskeletal health.
claims.claimText: Applicants must have a confirmed HSS host and serious interest in musculoskeletal health.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Host-confirmation precondition is a core observership eligibility criterion.

claims.quote: Due to a growing number of requests, applications are only accepted from individuals with a confirmed HSS host. To apply to the Academic Visitor Program, first secure a host. Have your host complete, sign, and return the host form to the Education Institute. When we receive your approved host form, an application will be emailed to you.
claims.claimText: Applicants must first secure a confirmed HSS host who returns a host form; application is then emailed.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Stepwise application route via host form submission to Education Institute.

claims.quote: Conferences: Adult Reconstruction & Joint Replacement, Anesthesiology, Foot & Ankle, Hand & Upper Extremity, Limb Lengthening & Complex Reconstruction, Metabolic Bone, Neurology, New York Bone Pathology, Pediatric Orthopaedics, Perioperative Medicine, Physiatry, Radiology & Imaging, Rheumatic Diseases, Rheumatology, Spine, Surgical Arthritis.
claims.claimText: Observership participants may attend conferences across orthopaedic and musculoskeletal subspecialties listed.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Specialty/conference list indicates breadth of clinical exposure available to observers.
